name: Thariq
summary: Announced Claude Code Channels (research preview) with Telegram and Discord MCP integrations, signaling chat-native remote control of coding sessions.

name: Josh Woodward
summary: Shared a Gemini Android voice UX fix so pauses no longer cut users off, improving practical multimodal assistant usability.

name: Aaron Levie
summary: Argued that workers who can effectively wield AI will create widening productivity and value gaps versus peers.

name: Amjad Masad
summary: Highlighted a tighter design-to-production loop where teams can explore many designs and then directly build into production workflows.

name: Ryo Lu
summary: Positioned Composer 2 around frontier-level intelligence with lower cost and speed advantages in AI coding workflows.

name: Dan Shipper
summary: Published model-vs-model workflow comparisons and practical guidance on AI-assisted writing quality and process optimization.

name: Garry Tan
summary: Argued Markdown + LLM is a high-leverage interface for context-heavy, intelligence-required work and complex task execution.

name: Peter Yang
summary: Framed OpenAI strategy as distribution-first via ChatGPT install base, then deeper coding utility to increase developer lock-in.

name: Zara Zhang
summary: Introduced MySay, a voice-first AI flow that interviews users and converts spoken thoughts into personalized social posts.

name: Benedict Evans
role: Guest on Data Driven NYC podcast
summary: Discussed OpenAI moat challenges and argued software value is shifting toward distribution, workflow ownership, and AI-enabled improvised software.
